name: CQC inspection preparation
description: Evidence gathering, mock inspections, action plans
current_process: Care home managers maintain a state of continuous inspection readiness, but the reality is that preparation intensifies dramatically when an assessment is anticipated or triggered. Under the Single Assessment Framework (introduced March 2024), CQC assessments now combine structured evidence gathering, service user feedback, workforce insights, and external data to make real-time judgements across five key questions (Safe, Effective, Caring, Well-led, Responsive). Managers must organise evidence against 34 quality statements — pulling together care plans, medication records, training files, policies, incident logs, governance meeting minutes, staff supervision records, complaints logs, and satisfaction surveys. Evidence is typically stored in ring binders or fragmented digital folders, sorted by key question and quality statement. Many homes commission external consultants to run mock inspections, simulating CQC visits and producing action plans that identify weaknesses. The Provider Information Return (PIR) requires managers to self-assess performance with supporting data. During the assessment itself, inspectors request specific documents at short notice, meaning staff must be able to locate and present evidence rapidly. Post-inspection, managers must produce and implement an action plan addressing any areas for improvement.
time_spent: Estimated 8–15 hours per month on continuous readiness activities (evidence filing, quality audits, governance meetings). Intensive preparation in the weeks before an anticipated inspection can consume 20–40+ hours of the registered manager's time, often involving evenings and weekends. Mock inspections by external consultants typically take 1–2 full days on-site. Action plan development and implementation post-inspection adds a further 10–20 hours. The CQC is currently completing approximately 500 assessments per month, well behind its target of 9,000 by September 2026 — meaning many providers face long periods of uncertainty about when their assessment will occur.
ai_solution: AI can transform CQC preparation from a panicked, paper-chasing exercise into a continuous, automated readiness system: (1) Automated evidence mapping — AI continuously scans the care home's digital records (care plans, incident logs, training records, policies, supervision notes) and automatically maps evidence to the relevant quality statement and key question, maintaining a live 'inspection-ready' evidence portfolio. (2) Gap analysis — AI identifies which quality statements lack sufficient evidence and alerts managers to areas needing attention before an inspector asks. (3) AI-powered mock inspections — automated assessment against the 34 quality statements, scoring the home's readiness and generating a prioritised action plan. (4) Auto-generated PIR responses from existing data, pre-populated with metrics, outcomes, and evidence references. (5) Instant document retrieval — when an inspector requests specific evidence, AI-powered search locates and presents relevant documents in seconds rather than hours of filing cabinet searches. (6) Trend dashboards showing improvement trajectories against each key question over time. ReflowAI offers AI-powered compliance automation that automates evidence capture and regulatory reporting for UK care homes.
buyer_language: "CQC could turn up any day, and I'm never quite sure if we've got everything in order. I spend hours pulling together evidence folders, running through checklists, worrying about what we might have missed. It's the thing that keeps me up at night. And after the inspection, there's another mountain of paperwork to get through the action plan."
outcome_statement: Inspection-ready every day of the year — with AI that continuously maps evidence to CQC quality statements, flags gaps before inspectors find them, and retrieves any document in seconds.
regulatory_alignment: Maps comprehensively across all five CQC key questions and 34 quality statements under the Single Assessment Framework. Most directly aligns with 'Governance, management and sustainability' (Well-led) — providers must demonstrate robust governance systems that use information about risks, performance, and outcomes to improve care. Also directly relevant to 'Learning, improvement and innovation' (Well-led). The CQC's 2025/26 business plan proposes simplifying scoring to quality-statement level and developing sector-specific frameworks, meaning evidence mapping will become even more critical.
existing_tech_gap: Current care management platforms (Nourish, Person Centred Software, Log my Care) store the underlying records but do not automatically map evidence to CQC quality statements or provide readiness scoring. QCS provides policy and compliance content but not automated evidence gathering. GoAudits offers CQC inspection checklists and templates but requires manual completion. ReflowAI is an emerging UK platform that automates compliance evidence capture and reporting. No mainstream platform currently offers AI-powered mock inspections, automated PIR generation, or predictive gap analysis against the 34 quality statements. AI adds unique value by connecting disparate data sources into a unified compliance picture and proactively identifying weaknesses.
implementation_complexity: Requires integration — AI evidence mapping needs access to the care home's care planning system, training records, HR system, incident reporting, and policy management. Homes using multiple disconnected systems will need API connectivity or data aggregation. Homes already using a unified digital care record platform will find implementation simpler. Process redesign is minimal — the AI overlays existing documentation rather than replacing it.
proof_of_concept_availability: Partial. ReflowAI offers a working AI compliance automation platform for UK care homes, reporting 40–60 admin hours saved per month. GoAudits provides digital CQC inspection checklists used by UK providers. Several consultancy firms (Curatum, Care Quality Support, DKJ Support Services) offer manual mock inspection services that could be augmented with AI. No fully AI-powered mock inspection product with automated scoring against all 34 quality statements exists yet in the UK market, representing a significant opportunity.
